title: RF-Benchmark-Hub reproducibility manifest
description: Reproducibility manifest accompanying a result.json in a Tier-2 (official) leaderboard PR. Declares everything the maintainer needs to re-run the evaluation and flip verification.status to 'verified'. Without these fields a result can only stay 'self_reported' (SUBMISSION.md). Minimal required set = code_commit + command + (weights_url OR docker_image) + hardware + expected_metrics + tolerance.

schema_version.description: SemVer of this submission schema. 1.0.0 (Sprint-0) or 1.1.0 (adds the `artifacts.source_only` form). Additive: 1.0.0 manifests keep validating unchanged.

result_path.description: Repo-relative path to the result.json this manifest reproduces. Regex-pinned so a PR self-locates under leaderboard/results/<task>/.

task.description: Must match the task block of the referenced result.json.

regime.description: Declared regime; MUST match the referenced result. Never inferred. `k_shot` REQUIRED iff few_shot, FORBIDDEN otherwise (allOf), mirroring result.schema.json.

code_commit.description: Exact git commit that produced the result. 'git@<sha>' or a bare 7-40 hex SHA (SUBMISSION.md). A dirty tree cannot be verified.

repo.description: Optional source repo URL if not this repo (fork).

command.description: Exact shell command that reproduces the result, e.g. 'rfbench eval amc --model mcldnn --regime full_finetune --dataset radioml_2018_01a --seed 42'.

artifacts.description: How the maintainer obtains the runnable environment. At least one of weights_url / docker_image / source_only is REQUIRED (anyOf): the default eval_only re-run needs fetchable weights; a full_retrain of a seed baseline may provide a pinned image; `source_only: true` covers a deterministic from-source baseline reproducible with no external artifact (schema 1.1.0).

weights_url.description: Resolvable URL to the checkpoint used (release asset, HF, Zenodo...). Enables the default eval_only re-run.

weights_checksum.description: SHA-256 of the weights artifact so the maintainer fetches the exact file.

docker_image.description: Pinned, digest-addressed image that froze the eval environment (risk: fragile old stacks, e.g. MCLDNN/TF1). 'name@sha256:...' form preferred.

source_only.description: The result is fully reproducible FROM SOURCE alone: `code_commit` + the exact `command` + the committed canonical split indices + the pinned `uv.lock` -- with no external weights or image to fetch (the D3 from-source ethos; `rfbench data prepare` re-downloads the dataset). Use for deterministic from_scratch / full_retrain seed baselines whose re-run needs no artifact; the maintainer re-runs the command at code_commit. Added in schema 1.1.0.

hardware.description: Hardware the submitter ran on, e.g. '1x NVIDIA A100 80GB'.

environment.description: Optional env fingerprint to rebuild the stack when Docker is absent.

pip_freeze_sha256.description: SHA-256 of the frozen dependency list (pip freeze / uv lock) archived alongside the submission.

expected_metrics.description: Metric values the maintainer's re-run must reproduce within `tolerance`. Keys mirror result.metrics.values; the task's primary metric MUST be present.

tolerance.description: Match criterion for the re-run. `absolute` (or `relative`) applies to the primary metric unless `per_metric` overrides. At least one bound required.

absolute.description: Absolute allowed delta on the primary metric, e.g. 0.005.

relative.description: Relative tolerance (fraction) on the primary metric.

per_metric.description: Optional per-metric absolute tolerances overriding the global bound.

rerun_mode.description: What the maintainer should do. Default eval_only (weights provided, D4 cost guard-rail); full_retrain for seed baselines.

data_provenance.description: Ties the run to the canonical, non-redistributed data (D3): only split id + checksums, never raw data.

manifest_checksum.description: SHA-256 of the dataset manifest emitted by core/manifest.py.

contact.description: Optional submitter contact (name/handle/email) for verification questions.

notes.description: Optional free-form notes (known caveats, dataset access instructions).
